pr.title: Try to fix CI race condition again
pr.author: ribbanya

summary: Merged the GitHub Pages generation/deploy workflow into the main build workflow to address a CI race condition tracked by #1270. The former standalone .github/workflows/publish-pages.yml was deleted, while the build workflow was renamed/updated to .github/workflows/build.yml and gained Pages permissions plus gen-pages and deploy-pages jobs chained after build-ninja.
what_changed: Renamed/updated the build workflow from .github/workflows/build-melee.yml to .github/workflows/build.yml.
Changed the workflow name from build-melee to build and the run-name to indicate both Melee build and GitHub Pages publishing.
Added workflow-level permissions for contents: read, id-token: write, packages: read, and pages: write.
Moved the GitHub Pages generation job into the build workflow as gen-pages.
Made gen-pages depend on build-ninja via needs: build-ninja, serializing page generation after the relevant build job.
Kept the existing Docker-based page generation flow: pull ghcr.io/doldecomp/melee/gen-pages:latest, mount the repository read-only at /input, mount output at /output, then upload the Pages artifact.
Added deploy-pages inside the build workflow, depending on gen-pages and gated to the repository default branch.
Added deploy-pages concurrency group deploy with cancel-in-progress: false.
smart_moves: Consolidating build and Pages publishing into one workflow gives GitHub Actions an explicit dependency edge, which is a direct way to reduce races between independent workflows.
Using needs: build-ninja for gen-pages documents that generated Pages should come from a completed build state rather than racing a parallel workflow.

The deploy step remains guarded with if: github.ref_name == github.event.repository.default_branch, preserving PR safety while still allowing generation/upload behavior to run in the unified workflow.
The Pages deployment uses GitHub's configure-pages, upload-pages-artifact, and deploy-pages actions rather than a custom deploy script.
The Docker generator invocation keeps host UID/GID mapping and read-only repository mounting, reducing artifact permission issues and avoiding accidental source tree mutation in CI.
decomp_lessons: For decompilation projects with generated progress pages or reports, tie publication jobs to the build job that produces/verifies the source state instead of letting a separate workflow race it.
If a generated site depends on build artifacts or build validation, encode that dependency with GitHub Actions needs rather than relying on separate workflow trigger timing.
CI infrastructure changes can be important decomp-enablers even when no C/ASM code changes: reliable Pages/progress output helps contributors evaluate matching progress.
Keep deployment gated to the default branch while still allowing PRs to exercise the generation path where safe.
When moving jobs between workflows, carry over required permissions explicitly; missing pages/id-token/packages permissions can create confusing CI failures.
naming_conventions: Workflow file/name was simplified from build-melee to build.
Job names use concise kebab-case identifiers such as build-make, gen-pages, and deploy-pages.
The generator image suffix is named gen-pages:latest and combined with IMAGE_REPO to form IMAGE.
Environment variables use uppercase names such as IMAGE_REPO, REGISTRY, OUTPUT, IMAGE_SUFFIX, and IMAGE.
review_feedback: No review comments or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR body only states: Fixes #1270; details of the underlying race are not included in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Look up issue #1270 to determine the exact CI race this PR fixed.
Search later PRs touching .github/workflows/build.yml for follow-up fixes to Pages deployment or build/publish ordering.
Check whether gen-pages depends on artifacts produced by build-ninja or only on repository checkout plus the generator image.
Verify whether the removed workflow-level concurrency group pages was intentionally replaced by deploy-pages concurrency group deploy only.
